title: Quiz: Introduction to QAOA
description: Test your understanding of the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm

questions.question: What does QAOA stand for?
questions.explanation: QAOA stands for Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm. It provides approximate solutions to combinatorial optimization problems using a hybrid quantum-classical approach.

questions.question: What is the role of the cost Hamiltonian in QAOA?

options.text: To encode the optimization problem objective

questions.explanation: The cost Hamiltonian C encodes the optimization problem. It is diagonal in the computational basis, with eigenvalues equal to the cost function values for each bitstring.

questions.question: What is the standard mixer Hamiltonian in QAOA?

options.text: Sum of Z operators: B = Σ Z_j
Sum of X operators: B = Σ X_j
Product of X operators: B = ∏ X_j

questions.explanation: The standard mixer Hamiltonian is B = Σⱼ Xⱼ. The X operators enable transitions between computational basis states by flipping bits.

questions.question: QAOA is guaranteed to find the optimal solution for any combinatorial problem.
questions.explanation: QAOA provides approximate solutions, not guaranteed optimal ones. The quality of the approximation depends on the depth p and the specific problem structure.

questions.question: What initial state is typically used in QAOA?

options.text: The ground state of the cost Hamiltonian

questions.explanation: QAOA starts from the uniform superposition |+⟩^n = H^⊗n|0⟩^n, which is the ground state of the standard mixer B = Σ Xⱼ.

questions.question: How is QAOA related to adiabatic quantum computing?

options.text: QAOA is a discretized version of adiabatic evolution
Adiabatic computing is a special case of QAOA
They use the same circuits but different measurements

questions.explanation: QAOA can be viewed as a discretized (Trotterized) version of adiabatic quantum computing, where the continuous evolution is broken into p discrete steps.

questions.question: What happens to QAOA performance as the depth p increases?

options.text: Performance generally improves or stays the same

questions.explanation: Higher depth p allows QAOA to better approximate optimal solutions. As p → ∞, QAOA approaches the adiabatic limit which can achieve exact solutions.

questions.question: Which are valid applications of QAOA? (Select all)
questions.explanation: QAOA is designed for combinatorial optimization problems like MaxCut (a), TSP (b), and portfolio optimization (c). Quantum chemistry (d) typically uses VQE instead.
